senses.definition: in way that continues to happen all the time with no stop

examples.x: She studies day and night to prepare for her exams.
He worries day and night about his financial situation.

senses.definition: at any given hour, particularly a time that is late

examples.x: She works all hours to meet the demands of her high-pressure job.
The convenience store is open all hours to cater to customers' needs.
He studies at all hours of the day and night to prepare for his exams.
They worked all hours to complete the project before the deadline.
The team practiced all hours leading up to the championship game.
If they want to succeed, they will have to work at all hours to meet their ambitious goals.

senses.definition: used for referring to a frequent change from something to another

examples.x: She adapts her plans from day to day based on the changing circumstances.
The stock market fluctuates from day to day, making it unpredictable for investors.
He manages his workload by prioritizing tasks from day to day.
The weather conditions varied from day to day during our vacation.
She adjusted her schedule from day to day to accommodate unexpected events.
If they want to succeed, they will need to adapt their strategies from day to day.
Nine times out of ten, she prefers tea over coffee in the morning.
In this game, nine times out of ten, the team with better preparation comes out victorious.
In our previous meetings, nine times out of ten, the client approved our proposals.
He remembered that, nine times out of ten, his grandmother would bake him cookies when he visited.
Nine times out of ten, they will select the candidate with the most experience for the job.

senses.definition: in a way that is not continuous or regular

examples.x: The internet connection at my house works off and on, causing occasional disruptions.
They meet up for coffee off and on, whenever their schedules align.
The weather forecast predicts rain off and on throughout the weekend.
They plan to travel off and on over the next year, taking breaks between trips.

senses.definition: used for saying that something can only happen on very rare occasions

examples.x: Once in a blue moon, she treats herself to a spa day for some much-needed relaxation.
They visit their grandparents' farm once in a blue moon to reconnect with their rural roots.
Once in a blue moon, he cooks a gourmet meal to impress his friends.
We went on a vacation together once in a blue moon, and it was a memorable experience.
Once in a blue moon, they attended a concert by their favorite band when they were in town.
Once in a blue moon, if the weather permits, they organize a neighborhood barbecue.

senses.definition: used for referring to a very special opportunity that most likely will not be offered more than once to someone

examples.x: They experience moments that happen only once in a lifetime.
He feels that this chance only comes once in a lifetime.
They witnessed a sight that happens only once in a lifetime.
If they win the competition, they would have an opportunity that comes once in a lifetime.
If she could meet her idol, it would be a moment that happens only once in a lifetime.

senses.definition: in a single action, without delay or hesitation

examples.x: The company's restructuring plan aims to cut costs and streamline operations at one fell swoop.
With his groundbreaking discovery, the scientist revolutionized the field of medicine at one fell swoop.
They are currently implementing a plan to address multiple issues in their organization at one fell swoop.
They completed the renovation of their house, repainted the walls, and replaced the flooring in one fell swoop.
In one fell swoop, they revolutionized the industry by introducing a groundbreaking technology.

senses.definition: in a manner that is consistent

examples.x: The convenience store is open twenty-four seven, allowing customers to shop at any time.
They offer customer support twenty-four seven, ensuring assistance is available around the clock.
The online streaming service provides access to movies and shows twenty-four seven.
The restaurant used to operate twenty-four seven, but they recently adjusted their hours.
They provided technical support twenty-four seven during the busy holiday season.
The website had frequent updates to ensure it was available twenty-four seven.
Time after time, he promises to change, but he never does.
This old truck has gotten me home time after time, no matter the weather.
Time after time, the same team wins the championship, it's getting boring!
I've told you time after time: don't leave the door unlocked!
That song takes me back time after time to our summer road trips.

senses.definition: used to describe something that happens at fixed intervals or follows a consistent pattern without fail

examples.x: The bus arrives at the stop, regular as clockwork, every morning at 8 a.m.
She waters her plants, regular as clockwork, every evening before sunset.
The mailman delivers the mail to our house, regular as clockwork, at noon.
The train departed from the station, regular as clockwork, despite the heavy snowfall.
He completed his daily workout routine, regular as clockwork, throughout the entire year.
